chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-379 of 2023 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on Office Objection. 2. For orders on Misc. No.1525/2023. 3. For hearing of main case. 07.02.2023. Ms. S. Rehana Naqvi, Advocate for the Petitioner. -------- YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J. - The Petitioner has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution, seeking that the Respondent No.1, a limited company operating in the private domain, be ordered to pay certain amount said to be due on account of the shareholding of the Petitioner’s forebear, in respect of whom the Petitioner has apparently obtained a Succession Certificate from the Competent Court of law. It is said that the amount claimed represents the value of the shares held by the deceased as well as accrued dividend in respect thereof from 2011 to 2013. Having heard the learned counsel for the Petitioner, we are of the view that the Petition is misconceived as a writ would not lie to enforce a claim against a private entity. If the Petitioner has any claim to advance on the basis of the Succession Certificate, he may invoke the jurisdiction of the concerned Civil Court to file an appropriate action for recovery of debt said to be recoverable from the Respondent No.1. Under the circumstances, the Petition stands dismissed along with the other miscellaneous application. JUDGE CHIEF JUSTICE MUBASHIR
